title: AliExpress Hit With Record $629 Million Fine For Selling Counterfeit And Illegal Products
excerpt: Europe just hit AliExpress with a record $629 million fine for allowing the sale of counterfeit and harmful products.
content_html:
Investigators found millions of counterfeit products, unsafe toys and dangerous cosmetics.

vfhnb12/Shutterstock

The European Commission (EC) hit Chinese e-commerce giant AliExpress with a record €550 million ($629 million) fine for allowing the sale of counterfeit and harmful products in the bloc. "The spread of counterfeit clothing, unsafe toys, dangerous cosmetics and other illegal and harmful products... is a failure by AliExpress to comply with its obligations under the Digital Services Act [DSA]," said EC's Executive Vice-President for Tech Sovereignty, Security and Democracy Henna Virkkunen in a press release.

The fine is the largest ever imposed under the DSA, which requires large platforms like AliExpress (owned by China's Alibaba) to police the spread of illegal and unsafe products and services. That lack of diligence "is very dangerous for our consumers, but it's also unfair for those companies that are complying with all our rules," Virkkunen added. 

The EC opened an investigation into AliExpress in March 2024 over suspected breaches to the DSA, including the platform's advertising and recommendation systems. Investigators found "millions" of counterfeit products, unsafe toys and dangerous cosmetics on sale even after being flagged. The EC determined that AliExpress didn't have enough staff to perform proper oversight, and that employees in charge of screening products sometimes had just "tens of seconds" to decide whether they met EU standards. 

The record fine reflects the scope of the problem and AliExpress's failure to take measures to stop it. "Scale is not an excuse; risks must be identified and addressed systematically to ensure consumers can safely shop online. Today, we are holding AliExpress to this standard and request it to take action," said Virkkunen.

AliExpress condemned the fine as disproportionate, though it didn't say whether it planned to appeal. "We disagree with today's decision and the disproportionate fine, which ‌does not adequately reflect our established framework and the significant, proactive enhancements we ⁠have made," the platform told The Guardian in a statement. "We are carefully reviewing the ‌decision and considering all available options."

Temu was fined €200 million ($232 million) under Europe's DSA in May, also for failing to prevent the sales of illegal products on its platform. The EU previously found that up to 65 percent of cosmetics, 63 percent of food supplements and 60 percent of personal protection equipment (like hard hats and steel-toe boots) on major platforms like Shein, Temu and AliExpress were non-compliant.

sentences.zh: 该平台在一份声明中告诉《卫报》：“我们不同意今天的决定和不成比例的罚款，这并不能充分反映我们既定的框架以及我们所做的重大、主动的改进。”

sentences.zh: “我们正在仔细审查该决定并考虑所有可用的选择。”

blocks.html_en: Temu was fined €200 million ($232 million) under Europe's DSA in May, also for failing to prevent the sales of illegal products on its platform. The EU previously found that up to 65 percent of cosmetics, 63 percent of food supplements and 60 percent of personal protection equipment (like hard hats and steel-toe boots) on major platforms like Shein, Temu and AliExpress were non-compliant.

sentences.en: Temu was fined €200 million ($232 million) under Europe's DSA in May, also for failing to prevent the sales of illegal products on its platform.
sentences.zh: 5 月份，Temu 因未能阻止非法产品在其平台上销售而被欧洲 DSA 处以 2 亿欧元（2.32 亿美元）罚款。

sentences.en: The EU previously found that up to 65 percent of cosmetics, 63 percent of food supplements and 60 percent of personal protection equipment (like hard hats and steel-toe boots) on major platforms like Shein, Temu and AliExpress were non-compliant.
sentences.zh: 欧盟此前发现，Shein、Temu、速卖通等主要平台上高达 65% 的化妆品、63% 的食品补充剂和 60% 的个人防护装备（如安全帽和钢头靴）不合规。

content_html_zh:
调查人员发现了数百万件假冒产品、不安全的玩具和危险化妆品。

vfhnb12/Shutterstock

欧盟委员会 (EC) 对中国电子商务巨头 AliExpress 处以创纪录的 5.5 亿欧元（6.29 亿美元）罚款，原因是允许在欧盟销售假冒和有害产品。 “假冒服装、不安全玩具、危险化妆品和其他非法有害产品的蔓延...... EC 负责技术主权、安全和民主的执行副总裁 Henna Virkkunen 在一份新闻稿中表示：“速卖通未能遵守《数字服务法》[DSA] 规定的义务。”

此次罚款是 DSA 有史以来最大的一笔罚款，该法案要求全球速卖通（中国阿里巴巴旗下）等大型平台监管非法和不安全产品和服务的传播。 维克库宁补充说，缺乏勤勉“对我们的消费者来说非常危险，但对那些遵守我们所有规则的公司来说也是不公平的”。

欧盟委员会于 2024 年 3 月对速卖通展开调查，原因是其涉嫌违反 DSA，包括该平台的广告和推荐系统。 调查人员发现，即使在被标记后，仍有“数百万”假冒产品、不安全的玩具和危险化妆品在销售。 欧盟委员会认定速卖通没有足够的员工来进行适当的监督，负责筛选产品的员工有时只有“几十秒”的时间来决定它们是否符合欧盟标准。

创纪录的罚款反映了问题的严重性以及全球速卖通未能采取措施阻止这一问题。 “规模不是借口；必须系统地识别和解决风险，以确保消费者可以安全地在线购物。 今天，我们要求全球速卖通遵守这一标准，并要求其采取行动，”Vikkunen 说。

速卖通谴责罚款不相称，但没有透露是否计划上诉。 该平台在一份声明中告诉《卫报》：“我们不同意今天的决定和不成比例的罚款，这并不能充分反映我们既定的框架以及我们所做的重大、主动的改进。” “我们正在仔细审查该决定并考虑所有可用的选择。”

5 月份，Temu 因未能阻止非法产品在其平台上销售而被欧洲 DSA 处以 2 亿欧元（2.32 亿美元）罚款。 欧盟此前发现，Shein、Temu、速卖通等主要平台上高达 65% 的化妆品、63% 的食品补充剂和 60% 的个人防护装备（如安全帽和钢头靴）不合规。
